data.title: Citizens projects 10%–12% NII growth and $700M–$850M share buybacks in 2026 while launching Reimagine the Bank
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Citizens Financial Group (CFG) Q4 2025

Management View

  • CEO Bruce Van Saun stated Citizens "were pleased to finish the year with another strong quarter," highlighting net interest margin expansion of 7 basis points, positive operating leverage, strong wealth and capital markets fees, and favorable credit trends.

  • Van Saun explained, "Our Private Bank finished the year with $14.5 billion in deposits, $10 billion in client assets and $7.2 billion in loans. The business was 7% accretive to pretax income in 2025, ahead of our 5% target. Importantly, we managed this business to a 25% ROE for the year." He noted the successful reduction of noncore assets and a $100 million run-rate benefit from their top 10 program.

  • Van Saun emphasized the launch of "Reimagine the Bank," a digital and operational transformation with "around 50 initiatives at the outset" intended to drive "meaningful enhancements to customer experience" and "real revenue benefits in addition to the targeted expense efficiency improvements."

  • He projected a favorable 2026 macroeconomic environment, expecting "solid GDP growth, stable unemployment and inflation falling by the end of the year," and stated, "Our focus for the foreseeable future remains on our attractive organic growth agenda."

  • CFO Aunoy Banerjee commented, "We delivered EPS of $3.86 for 2025, up 19% on an underlying basis, and that includes a $0.28 or just over 7% contribution from the Private Bank." Banerjee added, "Net interest income was up 4% as we delivered 13 basis points of margin expansion. Fees were up a strong 11% on an underlying basis, led by record results in both wealth, up 22% and Capital Markets, which had a nice pickup in the second half, up 9% year-over-year."

Outlook

  • Citizens projects "NII to be up 10% to 12% with NIM expanding about 4 to 5 basis points a quarter towards 3.25% in 4Q '26. Loan growth is expected to pick up this year with spot loans up 3% to 5%, average loans up 2.5% to 3.5% and overall earning assets up 4% to 5%."

  • Noninterest income is expected to rise "6% to 8%, driven primarily by wealth and capital markets." Expense growth is projected at 4.5% with "operating leverage in excess of 500 basis points."

  • Share repurchases are envisioned at "$700 million to $850 million" for 2026, and management will target a CET1 ratio of "10.5% to 10.6%."

  • Banerjee stated, "Credit is projected to continue to improve through the year with our outlook for net charge-offs in the mid- to high 30s basis points."

  • The Reimagine the Bank initiative is expected to have "about $50 million of front-loaded onetime costs that will be effectively offset by $45 million of benefits to be realized later in the year." The program targets "fully phased-in pretax run rate benefits of approximately $450 million as we exit 2028."

Financial Results

  • Citizens reported Q4 2025 EPS of $1.13, up 8% sequentially and 36% year-on-year. Net interest income for the quarter increased 3% sequentially, with net interest margin rising to 3.07%.

  • Wealth business delivered "another record quarter," up 5% sequentially and 31% year-over-year. Capital Markets recorded its "third best quarter ever, up 16% year-over-year."

  • Expenses rose 0.6% quarter-over-quarter, with the efficiency ratio improving to 62%. Average and period-end loans were up 1% (or 2% excluding noncore runoff). Total deposits increased to $183 billion, up 2% sequentially, with noninterest-bearing deposits at 22% of the mix.

  • Credit metrics improved, with net charge-offs at 43 basis points and allowance for credit losses at 1.53%. The company returned $326 million to shareholders in Q4 through dividends and buybacks.

  • The Private Bank contributed $0.10 to Q4 EPS and ended the year at $14.5 billion in deposits and $7.2 billion in loans. Tangible book value per share increased to $38.07, up 4% sequentially and 18% year-over-year.

Q&A

  • Ryan Nash, Goldman Sachs: Asked about the flow-through from the Reimagine the Bank program, and whether benefits would be reinvested or realized in the bottom line. Van Saun replied, "It's still a bit of an open question as to how much of that flows through... If we want to invest new dollars, try to figure out where to pinch the expense base to self-fund that. So I would expect that the flow-through should be high, but we're not going to make that call at this point."

  • L. Erika Penala, UBS: Inquired about loan growth guidance and balance sheet optimization. Van Saun highlighted "idiosyncratic growth driver in the Private Bank" and noted noncore runoff had lessened, with commercial and consumer loan growth supported by new products and regions.

  • Manan Gosalia, Morgan Stanley: Questioned fee growth assumptions and Capital Markets outlook. Van Saun acknowledged, "We had a very strong fee year in 2025," and described the 6%–8% fee growth guide as cautious due to potential market uncertainties.

  • Multiple analysts probed deposit growth, efficiency ratio targets, and details about Private Bank loan categories, with management emphasizing broad-based growth, deposit stability, and continued investments in the Private Bank.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts’ tone was slightly positive but pressed for granularity on expense management, Reimagine the Bank benefits, and the sustainability of loan and fee growth. Questions were direct and showed confidence in Citizens’ outlook, while expressing some caution around execution of large initiatives.

  • Management maintained a confident and optimistic tone in both prepared remarks and Q&A, frequently referencing successful execution and strong market positioning. Phrases such as "We are confident..." and "We expect..." appeared throughout. There was some measured caution, particularly on the timing and flow-through of cost savings.

  • Compared to the previous quarter, management’s tone remained upbeat, with additional optimism around new initiatives and Private Bank momentum. Analyst tone was similarly constructive, with some increased focus on execution risks and return timelines for announced programs.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Citizens provided significantly more detail on the Reimagine the Bank program in Q4, laying out specific cost, benefit, and timeline targets, whereas the previous quarter only hinted at its scope.

  • The outlook for loan and deposit growth was more assertive, with the Private Bank’s contributions and targets explicitly outlined for 2026 and beyond.

  • Operating leverage and efficiency ratio improvements are now more firmly quantified, with guidance for "operating leverage in excess of 500 basis points" and a mid-50s efficiency ratio medium-term target.

  • Analysts’ questions shifted toward the specifics of digital transformation, capital deployment, and Private Bank scalability compared to prior quarters’ broader strategic focus.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management noted execution risk around the Reimagine the Bank program, stating "we have a lot of wood to chop to actually execute this program."

  • Fee growth guidance was described as "cautious" given market and regulatory uncertainties.

  • Credit risk remains under watch, particularly in CRE, but Van Saun and Banerjee reported improving trends and robust allowance coverage.

  • Analysts flagged the sustainability of high Private Bank growth, the pace of deposit accumulation, and expense discipline as areas to monitor.

Final Takeaway

Citizens Financial Group closed 2025 with strong operating momentum, record Private Bank growth, and improved credit metrics. Management is projecting robust NII and loan growth for 2026, driven by continued Private Bank expansion, positive market conditions, and the large-scale Reimagine the Bank transformation. While the company expects significant operating leverage and capital returns, management emphasized disciplined execution and ongoing investments in strategic growth areas to sustain performance and achieve medium-term ROTCE targets.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: T1 Energy falls amid short call from Culper Research
data.description:
• T1 Energy (TE) dropped 13% at least partly amid a short call from Culper Research.
  • Culper Research disclosed that it's short shares of T1 Energy (TE).
  • T1  Energy didn't immediately respond to Seeking Alpha's email request for comment.
  • T1 Energy has short interest of 8.6%.

data.title: Distillate Capital Partners announces Q4 2025 portfolio changes
data.description:
• Distillate Capital Partners announced portfolio changes at the U.S. Fundamental Stability & Value, U.S. Small/Mid Cap Quality & Value, and International Fundamental Stability & Value as part of the Q4 2025 rebalancing.
  • U.S. Fundamental Stability & Value Composite purchased Bristol-Myers Squibb (BMY), Vistra (VST), and TE Connectivity (TEL) and sold Elevance Health (ELV), HCA Healthcare (HCA), and Lowe's Companies (LOW).
  • U.S. FSV Strategy increased its stake in Uber Technologies (UBER) (UBER:CA) (ZUBE:CA), T-Mobile US (TMUS), and Marathon Petroleum (MPC), while trimming its position in Johnson & Johnson (JNJ), Merck & Co. (MRK), and Cisco Systems (CSCO) (CSCO:CA).
  • U.S. Small/Mid Cap Quality & Value Composite bought CF Industries Holdings (CF), Molson Coors Beverage (TAP) (TAP.A), and Toll Brothers (TOL); and exited Range Resources (RRC), Gap (GAP), and TopBuild (BLD).
  • U.S. SMID QV Strategy added HF Sinclair (DINO), Ovintiv (OVV), and Owens Corning (OC), and cut its holdings in DNOW (DNOW), Steven Madden (SHOO), and Pediatrix Medical Group (MD).
  • International Fundamental Stability & Value Composite acquired Agnico Eagle Mines (AEM) (AEM:CA), HD HYUNDAI, and Rio Tinto (RIO) (RTPPF) (RTNTF), and divested from SK Hynix (HXSC.F), LVMH Moët Hennessy (LVMHF) (LVMUY) (LV:CA), and Aluminum Corporation of China (ALMMF).
  • INTL FSV Strategy added to its stake in JD.com (JD) (JDCMF), CGI (GIB), and JD Logistics (JDLGF), while reducing its position in Roche (RHHBY) (RHHBF) (RHHVF), British American Tobacco (BTI) (BTAFF), and Fomento Económico Mexicano (FMX).
  • Source: Fund Letter

data.title: EU delays vote on US trade deal, is prepared to respond to tariff threat
data.description:
The European Parliament has delayed its vote on its trade deal with the U.S. in response to President Donald Trump's threat to impose additional tariffs on any countries that oppose the U.S. taking over Greenland, according to media reports. 

The EU's trade committee was scheduled to set its position in votes on Jan. 26-27, but that has been delayed. The committee chair, Bernd Lange, said that the threat of new tariffs has upended a trade deal that was reached in Scotland in July.

In a speech to the parliament on Wednesday, EU President Ursula von der Leyen objected to the potential new tariffs, citing the bloc's cooperation with the U.S. on ensuring security in the Arctic region.

"We are aligned. We are working together. We share the same strategic assessment on Arctic Security," she said. "And this is why the threat of additional tariffs for security reasons is simply wrong." 

And even with the EU's history of cooperation with the U.S., the EU can't stand by while the U.S. acts on its own.

"We are at a crossroads," von der Leyen said. "Europe prefers dialogue and solutions – but we are fully prepared to act, if necessary, with unity, urgency, and determination."

Later on Wednesday, Trump insisted that the U.S. is the only nation that can properly protect Greenland, ensuring global security. He, however, said he would not use force to gain control of the semi-autonomous island that's part of Denmark.

The EU will work with the U.S. and all partners on wider Arctic security, she said. But the allied nations must have their own approach. That's why it's working on a package to support Arctic security, which includes a "a massive European investment surge" in Greenland.

Von der Leyen pointed to progress in reaching trade agreements in multiple regions, including a deal with the Mercosur alliance in South America and an agreement with India to be signed next week. "And negotiations are advancing with many others who want to do business with us," she said.

As those trade deals open opportunities for the EU, they'll create jobs in Europe, reinforcing resilience and security of supply. "Because the more trading partners we have worldwide, the more independent we are," von der Leyen said.

EU officials have prepared retaliation measures if the U.S. imposes additional tariffs, including what's known as the "bazooka," which would limit U.S. tech companies' access to the EU market.

Europe ETFs: (VGK), (EZU), (BBEU), (DBEF), (IEUR), (FEZ), (EWU), (HEDJ), (IEV)

Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: Ulta and Sally Beauty are standouts in the HPC category in 2026 — Raymond James
data.description:
The beauty, personal care, and household products sector enters 2026 facing a challenging backdrop, marked by slowing global consumption, a persistent K-shaped recovery, uneven momentum in China, and tariffs that have become a structural cost headwind. Offsetting these pressures, restructuring initiatives launched in 2025 are expected to begin delivering benefits in 2026.

Within this backdrop, Raymond James analyst Olivia Tong expects high-growth categories like beauty, and higher-quality companies that can “weather the storm and offer meaningfully different initiatives” to outperform, leading Tong to raise Ulta Beauty (ULTA) to a Strong Buy from Outperform in anticipation of “outsized growth in 2026 after a period of heavy investments,” and Sally Beauty Holdings (SBH) to Outperform from Market Perform and a top small-medium cap value pick, expecting “faster and more consistent growth as store improvements roll out.”

Tong considers Ulta (ULTA) a “premium retailer worthy of a premium multiple” and praises the company’s effort to engage younger customers, its Ultamate loyalty program (which accounts for ~95% of sales), and the Replenish & Save program that locks in recurring purchases.

Tong also commends Ulta’s (ULTA) effort to lure in younger shoppers with its Rare Beauty by Selena Gomez collection and separate teen accounts that enable the company to better understand and engage that demographic.

With Sally Beauty Holdings (SBH), management’s efforts to rationalize underperforming SKUs and explore near-neighbor categories to increase “share of wallet” drives Tong’s Outperform rating and top-pick designation.

“We see the 'Sally Ignited' store refreshes as a central pillar to the story,” Tong says, adding that new initiatives and DoorDash (DASH) and Uber Eats (UBER) partnerships “prioritize customer convenience.”

And like Ulta (ULTA), Sally Beauty (SBH) is also raising its profile with Millennials and Gen Z through increased engagement, value, and improvements to online buying that include 2-hour delivery in select markets.

Along with a pair of upgrades, Tong raised her price target for Ulta (ULTA) by 31% to $790 and set a $19 price target for Sally’s Beauty (SBH).

The upgrades contributed to today’s multi-year highs for both Ulta (ULTA) and Sally Beauty Holdings (SBH) with the former setting another record high and the latter trading +4% to its highest level in nearly two years.

data.title: Market Voices: Trump on Greenland, Supreme Court on Fed, IPO market
data.description:
Seeking Alpha's roundup of statements, announcements, and remarks that could impact markets, sectors, or individual stocks.

  • President Trump told world leaders at the World Economic Forum in Davos, Switzerland, that he wants “immediate negotiations” with Denmark to discuss a U.S. takeover of Greenland but ruled out using military force to seize the island.

“People thought I would use force. I don’t have to use force. I don’t want to use force. I won’t use force," Trump said during an address at Davos, according to CNBC. 

  • Supreme Court Justice Brett Kavanaugh said the Trump administration's assertion that it has the authority to fire Federal Reserve Governor Lisa Cook without any judicial review would jeopardize the independence of the central bank. 

"Your position that there's no judicial review, no process required, no remedy available, very low bar for cause that the president alone determines — I mean, that would weaken, if not shatter, the independence of the Federal Reserve," Kavanaugh told Solicitor General D. John Sauer during a hearing on the case Wednesday, according to Reuters. Sauer was presenting the Trump administration's arguments in the case.

During the hearing, Justice Amy Coney Barrett also questioned why the Trump administration denied Cook a hearing to defend herself against the accusations, Reuters added.

The Supreme Court is expected to issue a decision in the coming weeks on whether the Trump administration has the authority to remove Cook without a hearing over accusations that she engaged in mortgage fraud. Cook has denied any wrongdoing and is fighting her ouster in court. 

  • NYSE (ICE) President Lynn Martin said investors can expect to see a “tremendous” amount of IPO activity over the next few months.

“We have a deal on the road at the moment; we have another deal going next week,” Martin told Bloomberg TV during an interview at Davos. “We really see it towards the back end of Q1 into Q2."

data.title: State Street, QNB team up for new custody servicing model in Qatar
data.description:
Custodial lender State Street (STT) and QNB Group, the largest Qatari financial institution, are teaming up to introduce a new custody servicing model in Qatar, the companies said Wednesday.

The co‑operation agreement was signed during the World Economic Forum in Davos, Switzerland, expanding State Street's (STT) market position within the Gulf Cooperation Council ("GCC") and supporting QNB's international growth.

“The GCC is one of the fastest-growing markets globally, and we are committed to deepening our presence through collaboration with leading local institutions,” said State Street Chairman and CEO Ron O’Hanley. “This new servicing model creates significant synergies, enabling us to deliver enhanced solutions to clients across the region. QNB’s scale and network in Qatar are important to State Street as we invest further to support GCC clients in capturing opportunities driven by the region’s economic transformation,” he added.

State Street (STT) has been servicing sovereign wealth funds, pension schemes, central banks, and asset managers in the Middle East for over three decades, with offices in Abu Dhabi, Dubai, Muscat and Riyadh. It has a total of $12.9T in assets under custody / administration and in Europe, the Middle East and Africa. QNB has some $65B in assets under custody.

Meanwhile, the Middle East has become a key and growing funding source for private capital managers like Goldman Sachs (GS), which said earlier in the week it's deepening its partnership with Qatar's sovereign wealth fund, Qatar Investment Authority.

data.title: Japan's Tepco restarts its first nuclear reactor since 2011 Fukushima disaster
data.description:
Tokyo Electric Power (TKECF) (TKECY) on Wednesday restarted a reactor at the world's largest nuclear power plant, the Japanese utility's first such move since the Fukushima disaster in 2011 and a landmark in the global return back to atomic energy.

Tepco (TKECF) (TKECY) said it started the No. 6 reactor, one of seven reactors at its Kashiwazaki-Kariwa plant in Niigata prefecture after completing an inspection of the reactor's control rods.

Kashiwazaki-Kariwa was among 54 reactors that were shut down following the 2011 earthquake and tsunami that crippled Fukushima Daiichi; Japan has restarted 14 of the 33 reactors that remain operable, as it tries to wean itself off imported fossil fuels, but this is the first Tepco-run unit to resume production.

The plant's total capacity is 8.2 GW, and the pending restart brings the 1.36 GW No. 6 unit online, with a potential restart of another unit with the same capacity as soon as 2029.

ETFs: (NLR), (URA), (URNM)

data.title: Corporate AI adoption could take decades, longer than 10-15 years
data.description:
Orlando Bravo, founder and managing partner at private equity giant Thoma Bravo, believes corporate adoption of artificial intelligence will unfold over multiple decades rather than years.

His timeline extends beyond even the 10–15-year projection recently offered by hedge fund manager Ken Griffin, signaling that expectations for rapid enterprise transformation may be overblown.

“The corporate world once again is evolutionary, not revolutionary, and it works slowly over time in terms of its adoption of new technologies,” Bravo said in an interview with CNBC.

He agreed with Griffin’s assessment but suggested the timeline could be “even longer,” characterizing AI integration as a gradual process rather than an overnight shift.

Despite the extended adoption timeline, Bravo views AI as a massive long-term opportunity for the software industry. He described the current infrastructure buildout as “much bigger than cloud, more transformative than cloud,” calling it “an incredible boom to enterprise software” and a “multi-decade growth opportunity.”

Thoma Bravo plans to capitalize on this extended runway by becoming “a lot more active” in acquiring both public and private companies, he added. Bravo noted that lower private equity multiples in software have created favorable conditions for large software vendors to consolidate their markets, broadening their platforms to better serve customers in an AI-driven landscape.

The investor emphasized the enduring value of domain expertise within established software companies, arguing that this specialized knowledge “cannot be copied by an AI code.”

He pointed to ServiceNow (NOW) and Salesforce (CRM) as “phenomenal consolidators” and cited Thoma Bravo’s portfolio company Anaplan as an example, with ambitions to consolidate the market and potentially grow the company’s value to between $50B and $70B.

AI and robotics ETFs: (AIQ), (BOTZ), (DTEC), (WTAI), (XAIX), (WISE), (GINN), (ROBT), (TECB), (XT), (THNQ), (CHAT)

Semiconductor ETFs: (SMH), (SOXX), (SOXL), (FTXL), (XSD), (USD), (PSI), (SEMI)

Cloud computing ETFs: (SKYY), (WCLD), (CLOU), (FCLD), (SKYU), (CLOD)

data.title: SA analyst upgrades/downgrades: AMD, ACN, LULU, NFLX
data.description:
Recent analyst actions include Accenture (ACN) receiving an upgrade as bookings rebound and GenAI demand accelerates, while lululemon (LULU) was upgraded to Hold amid CEO transition hopes despite shaky fundamentals. On the downgrade side, AMD (AMD) faces valuation concerns despite strong AI sentiment, and Netflix (NFLX) continues its slide as analysts argue the stock price must adjust to reality.

Upgrades

  • Accenture (ACN): Upgrade Hold to Buy by Redfox Capital Ideas. The analyst upgraded based on a massive bookings rebound, with new bookings up 12% year-over-year to $20.9 billion, and GenAI bookings now exceeding 10% of total bookings.

    “Bookings have recovered nicely, the mix has improved, and GenAI bookings are becoming a more tangible growth driver. With valuation still undemanding relative to the market, I believe the risk-reward has turned positive. … ACN still trades near its 10-year low, at a ~10% discount to the S&P 500, but the operating data has clearly shown a solid recovery.”

  • lululemon athletica (LULU): Upgrade Sell to Hold by Bay Area Ideas. The analyst sees improved risk/reward due to upcoming CEO transition and aggressive international expansion plans, despite continued fundamental weakness.

    “While lululemon’s fundamentals are currently weaker than desired, there are some good reasons for investors to be hopeful. … A CEO transition is not an easy thing per se, and some headwinds like tariffs will be hard to mitigate in the near term. Therefore, a cautious stance right now will likely pay off, and I believe investors should avoid jumping the gun.”

Downgrades

  • Advanced Micro Devices (AMD): Downgrade Buy to Hold by Oliver Rodzianko. The analyst cites extreme market valuations and warns that the AI capex cycle is near peak sentiment, suggesting now is the time to trim positions rather than add exposure.

    “When I look at AMD, I see a genuinely remarkable company and CEO. But I also see a crowd of market participants that have projected their value onto the stock to the detriment of the equity’s intrinsic valuation being accurately depicted in public markets. … A consensus rating of +23.5% upside in the next 12 months means nothing in the face of an aggregate market valuation that is more than +2 standard deviations above fair value.”

  • Netflix (NFLX): Downgrade to Sell by Long Player. The analyst argues that despite earnings and cash flow growth, the current valuation implies unsustainable 30%+ annual growth, with potential for 50% more downside.

    “The business is fine. It is the stock price that needs adjusting. Mr. Market is coming out of one of those periods of irrational exuberance that has marked this stock price since it went public. … The latest earnings report continues a growth rate that is expected for a large company like this that is nothing close to that price-earnings ratio.”
